17	// TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement verifies that a
18	// replacement description can remove stale/noisy old prose when explicit
19	// commitments and durable anchors are preserved. (AC1)
20	func TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement(t *testing.T) {
21		original := &bead.Bead{
22			ID: "ddx-test0001",
23			Description: "PROBLEM\nThe old intake system duplicated stale chat noise in every bead prompt." +
24				" Extra: references to slack thread 2025-01, per internal RFC-47.\n\n" +
25				"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces append-only check\n\n" +
26				"PROPOSED FIX\nReplace substring check per FEAT-010 and ADR-023\n\n" +
27				"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n",
28			Acceptance: "1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n",
29		}
30		// Compressed replacement: drops noisy prose, keeps all explicit commitments.
31		compressedDesc := "PROBLEM\nAppend-only check rejects valid replacements per FEAT-010 and ADR-023.\n\n" +
32			"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces substring containment\n\n" +
33			"PROPOSED FIX\nReplace with commitment-based validation\n\n" +
34			"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n"
35	
36		rewrite := PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
37			Description:   compressedDesc,
38			ChangedFields: []string{"description"},
39		}
40	
41		got, _, _, preservation, err := validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite(original, rewrite)
42		require.NoError(t, err)
43		assert.Equal(t, strings.TrimSpace(compressedDesc), got.Description)
44		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "FEAT-010")
45		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "ADR-023")
46		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119")
47		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "non_scope:Do not change acceptance criteria validator")
48		// Stale prose is gone from the replacement.
49		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "slack thread 2025-01")
50		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "RFC-47")

15	func compileBeadSchema(t *testing.T) *jsonschema.Schema {
16		t.Helper()
17	
18		_, currentFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
19		require.True(t, ok)
20	
21		schemaPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(currentFile), "schema", "bead-record.schema.json")
22		raw, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)
23		require.NoError(t, err)
24	
25		compiler := jsonschema.NewCompiler()
26		require.NoError(t, compiler.AddResource("bead-record.schema.json", strings.NewReader(string(raw))))
27	
28		schema, err := compiler.Compile("bead-record.schema.json")
29		require.NoError(t, err)
30		return schema
31	}
32	
33	func validateJSONAgainstSchema(t *testing.T, schema *jsonschema.Schema, raw string) error {
34		t.Helper()
35		var v any
36		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &v))
37		return schema.Validate(v)
38	}
39	
40	func TestBeadRecordSchemaValidatesBdExportExample(t *testing.T) {
41		schema := compileBeadSchema(t)
42	
43		bdExport := `{"id":"bd-test-1x4","title":"test issue","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"user@example.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","created_by":"Test User","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-test-1x4","depends_on_id":"bd-test-ioz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T21:23:54Z","created_by":"Test User","metadata":"{}"}],"dependency_count":1,"dependent_count":0,"comment_count":0}`
44	
45		require.NoError(t, validateJSONAgainstSchema(t, schema, bdExport))
46	}

data.text: I need to add the helper function to compile the schema. Let me update the test file:

input.new_string:
package agent

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io/ioutil"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v5"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func compileReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) *jsonschema.Schema {
	t.Helper()

	_, currentFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
	require.True(t, ok)

	schemaPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(currentFile), "schema", "readiness-checks.schema.json")
	raw, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	compiler := jsonschema.NewCompiler()
	require.NoError(t, compiler.AddResource("readiness-checks.schema.json", strings.NewReader(string(raw))))

	schema, err := compiler.Compile("readiness-checks.schema.json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	return schema
}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Lock readiness_checks JSON schema with CI drift detection [ddx-52d1c006]

Define canonical readiness_checks schema at cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json
with verdict as oneOf boolean|string|null. The producer prompt and consumer decoder now both
reference this shared schema via readinessChecksSchemaPath constant to prevent silent contract drift.

Add TestReadinessChecksSchema which validates representative payloads (bool true/false, string,
null, omitted) decode correctly per the readinessVerdict UnmarshalJSON coercion contract. Test
fails on drift between code and schema file, catching the silent verdict=bool/string mismatch
that idled the fizeau queue.

Fix TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema to validate only the 
readiness_checks field against the schema (not the entire payload).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Perfect! All acceptance criteria are satisfied:

**AC1 ✓** — Schema is defined at `cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json` with `verdict` as `oneOf boolean|string|null`. The `TestReadinessChecksSchema` test validates it exists, is valid JSON, and enforces the schema constraint.

**AC2 ✓** — The consumer decoder at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21` defines the `readinessChecksSchemaPath` constant that both producer and consumer reference. Code comments in `readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON` (lines 84–94) and `preClaimReadinessChecksPayload` (lines 377–378) explicitly state they must stay aligned with the schema. The test fails on drift.
